answer:
The following works in the Falsalfa corpus contain explicit references to prayer (direct acts of praying, quoted prayers, or ritual prayers addressed to deities) or invocation (formal calling upon gods, ancestors, or spirits in ritual or poetic contexts). This is determined from full-text searches and chapter scans; mere metaphorical or incidental mentions (e.g., "prayer beads" as objects) are excluded. Works are listed alphabetically by slug, with one representative explicit example cited per work (via targeted passages). Note that poetry collections often use prayer poetically, while Indic Smṛtis describe ritual prayers/invocations prescriptively. No other works in the 37-work corpus match.[^1]

**cynewulf-andreas-07b573 (*Andreas*)**: Multiple Christian prayers to God, including a quoted prayer for divine aid answered from heaven.[^2]

**cynewulf-elene-d2d132 (*Elene*)**: Explicit instruction to "send up your prayer into that bright creation" to the Glory-King (God).[^3]

**cynewulf-juliana-9a2157 (*Juliana*)**: Describes standing "bold in prayer" against demonic temptation, with the devil admitting he turns men from "the place of prayer."[^4]

**allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c (*Bang-E-Dara Part 1*)**: Contains a full chapter "Bachay Ki Dua" ("A Child's Prayer"), explicitly addressing God.[^5]

**allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c (*Bang-E-Dara Part 2*)**: Ritualistic "prayer, offered in its flame" around the cup as a sacred rite.[^6]

**allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8 (*Bang-E-Dara Part 3*)**: Multiple invocations, e.g., prayer-calls echoing in foreign lands and battle.[^7]

**mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c (*Diwan-E-Ghalib*)**: Poetic prayers, e.g., "a spell of prayer is no match for an arduous quest; O Lord, accept my plea."[^8]

**sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798 (*Diwan-E-Zauq*)**: "My brow is bowed upon her threshold-stone... The stone itself my prayer-rug."[^9]

**unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1 (*Āṅgirasa Smṛti*)**: Ritual twilight prayers (*sandhyā*) performed mentally or in water; reciting prayers to a secondary wife.[^10]

**unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136 (*Gaṇapatitattva*)**: Invocation mantra: "O [deity]... sprinkle it on the place afflicted by a spirit."[^11]

**unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a (*Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*)**: Worship with quoted prayer to Lord Sugata.[^12]

**unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be (*Parāśara Smṛti*)**: Twilight prayers, bathing, and recitation (*japa*) as purification.[^13]

**unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8 (*Viṣṇu Smṛti*)**: Multiple ritual prayers, e.g., muttering *aghamarṣaṇa* (sin-effacing prayer) or twilight worship.[^14]

[^1]: Full corpus search via `search_corpus` for "prayer", "pray", "invocation", "invoke" (limits 100); cross-checked with `list_works` (37 total) and wiki cards for Cynewulf/Iqbal. No matches in remaining works (e.g., no explicit prayers in Comte/Dunoyer economic treatises or other Smṛtis).
[^2]: Cynewulf, *Andreas*, [p-bdbda7](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-bdbda7).
[^3]: Cynewulf, *Elene*, [p-f5d1f1](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-f5d1f1).
[^4]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [p-3d4216](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-3d4216).
[^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [p-28b7ba](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/16-bachay-ki-dua/translation/#p-28b7ba).
[^6]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [p-f730e9](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/03-misaal-e-partav-e-mai-tawaf-e-jaam-karte-hain/translation/#p-f730e9).
[^7]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, [p-e1af7a](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/#p-e1af7a).
[^8]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [p-018190](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/68-hareef-e-matlab-e-mushkil-nahin-fusoon-e-niyaaz/translation/#p-018190).
[^9]: Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq*, [p-3f09b9](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/57-iss-sang-e-aastaan-peh-jabeen-e-niyaaz-hai/translation/#p-3f09b9).
[^10]: Unknown, *Āṅgirasa Smṛti*, [p-8ae8df](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-8ae8df).
[^11]: Unknown, *Gaṇapatitattva*, [p-36c962](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-36c962).
[^12]: Unknown, *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, [p-0a12ac](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-0a12ac).
[^13]: Unknown, *Parāśara Smṛti*, [p-40daa2](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-40daa2).
[^14]: Unknown, *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [p-48ae1a](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/28/translation/#p-48ae1a).
